properties.name: SEA-ME-WE 5
properties.wounding_strategy: Digital Financial Gateway: As nations rapidly digitize financial ecosystems, they become hyper-reliant on real-time server handshakes across continents. A coordinated grey-zone disruption in the Mediterranean or Arabian Sea segments would freeze international digital payments, paralyzing gig economies and stock markets for hours or days.

properties.name: SEA-ME-WE 6
properties.wounding_strategy: Highest-capacity Asia-Europe link. Passes through Djibouti corridor adjacent to Houthi operational zone. Single cut near Bab al-Mandab would sever Southeast Asia from European financial systems, disrupting $2.5T in daily forex transactions.

properties.name: MAREA (Microsoft/Facebook)
properties.wounding_strategy: Trans-Atlantic backbone for Big Tech cloud infrastructure. Disruption would disconnect US hyperscale data centers from European users. Microsoft Azure and Meta depend on this for real-time content delivery to 500M+ European users. Target for state-level disruption to fracture digital NATO cohesion.

properties.name: 2Africa Cable
properties.wounding_strategy: World's longest submarine cable at 45,000km circumnavigating Africa. Backed by Meta, Google, Vodafone. Connects 3 billion potential internet users across Africa. Destruction at any of 46 landing points would leave entire African nations digitally dark. Chinese-built port infrastructure near several landing points creates dual intelligence access.

properties.name: PEACE Cable
properties.wounding_strategy: Pakistan-East Africa-Europe cable funded with Chinese telecom investment. Provides alternative routing for Chinese data that bypasses US-controlled cable paths. Passes through Djibouti where China has a naval base. Intelligence concern: cable landing stations in Pakistan and Djibouti accessible to Chinese state intelligence.

properties.name: AAG (Asia-America Gateway)
properties.wounding_strategy: Primary Pacific cable linking Southeast Asia, Japan, and US West Coast. Carries massive trans-Pacific data including Amazon Web Services traffic. PLAN surface and submarine vessels operate along its route. A coordinated severance near the Philippine Sea would isolate South Korea, Philippines, and Vietnam from US digital infrastructure during a Taiwan crisis.

properties.name: JUPITER (Google)
properties.wounding_strategy: Google-owned direct US-Japan cable bypassing older multi-party cables. Critical for Google Cloud Japan operations and US-Japan financial market data synchronization. Route passes near disputed Senkaku/Diaoyu area. Japan-US alliance data dependency creates a priority target in any PLAN conflict scenario.

properties.name: New Cross Pacific (NCP)
properties.wounding_strategy: NCP passes through Hong Kong, creating a Chinese state intelligence access point for traffic between Southeast Asia and the US. US intelligence has flagged this cable's Hong Kong landing station as a potential intercept point. Ongoing geopolitical pressure to reroute away from Hong Kong.

properties.name: Curie Cable (Google)
properties.wounding_strategy: Google-owned cable serving South America. Passes through Panama Canal approaches — a dual chokepoint. Chile anchor point provides critical South American financial market connectivity. Disruption would isolate South American financial systems from US and Asia markets simultaneously, amplifying economic pressure on US allies.

properties.name: Marseille Landing Hub
properties.wounding_strategy: Europe's largest cable landing station processing internet traffic for 750M European users. Sabotage via anchor drag, undersea ROV, or land-side cyberattack would sever 15+ trans-continental data links simultaneously. Marseille Port proximity gives hostile vessels plausible maritime access.

properties.name: Mumbai (Versova) Landing Station
properties.wounding_strategy: South Asia's primary internet gateway handling data for 1.4 billion Indians. All major international financial connections to India route through here. Pakistan-backed cyber or kinetic attacks on this single station would functionally sever India from the global financial system, halting Unified Payments Interface (UPI) international transactions.

properties.name: Singapore Changi Cable Station
properties.wounding_strategy: Asia-Pacific's most important cable hub. 25+ cables land here connecting all of Asia to the Middle East, Europe, and Pacific. Singapore's neutrality is its strategic protection — but a PLAN blockade of Malacca Strait would physically isolate the station's seafloor approaches, severing cable maintenance access and enabling controlled degradation of Asian internet.

properties.name: Hong Kong Cable Landing
properties.wounding_strategy: After National Security Law (2020), Hong Kong cable stations are under de facto PRC intelligence jurisdiction. Traffic surveillance possible for 12+ cables transiting the station. Western telecoms have begun rerouting sensitive cables to bypass Hong Kong. However, existing cable infrastructure creates persistent intelligence access to Asia-Pacific internet backbone.

properties.name: Tokyo Shima Cable Station
properties.wounding_strategy: Japan's primary Pacific cable gateway. PLAN cyber operations have repeatedly probed Japanese telecommunications infrastructure. Eight cables including JUPITER and several Pacific crossings land here. In a Taiwan Strait conflict, this station becomes a priority target for PLAN cyber operations to sever US-Japan digital alliance coordination.

properties.name: Alexandria Cable Hub
properties.wounding_strategy: Mediterranean-Red Sea junction point where all Asia-Europe cables must transit through Egyptian territory. Egyptian state intelligence has access to all landing stations. Political instability (IMF debt crisis, military governance) creates insider threat risk. A hostile state paying Egyptian military officials could gain access to wiretap 12 international cables simultaneously.

properties.name: Djibouti Cable Node
properties.wounding_strategy: Nine cables transit this tiny nation at the mouth of the Red Sea. Djibouti hosts military bases for US, France, China, Japan, and Italy simultaneously — the only place on Earth where US and Chinese military bases are within 10km of each other. Chinese PLA base has direct proximity to cable landing stations, creating persistent intelligence collection opportunity.

properties.name: Virginia Beach Landing Station
properties.wounding_strategy: Primary US East Coast internet gateway and MAREA anchor point. NSA signals intelligence collection historically conducted at this facility. Russian GRU cyber units have been documented probing US cable landing infrastructure. Physical attack on above-ground cable vaults would be highest-impact, lowest-attribution cyber-physical attack possible against US internet infrastructure.

properties.name: Porthcurno (Cornwall) Station
properties.wounding_strategy: UK's Atlantic cable gateway in remote Cornwall. A rogue commercial fishing trawler dragging its anchor over the cable approaches — plausibly accidental — would sever multiple transatlantic links. Banking in London could be disrupted for days. Russian shadow fleet vessels have been observed operating near UK cable approaches in the Irish Sea and Western Approaches.

properties.name: Fortaleza Cable Hub (Brazil)
properties.wounding_strategy: South America's primary internet hub. Cables here connect to West Africa (2Africa), US East Coast, and Europe. Disruption would isolate Brazil's $2T economy from global financial systems. São Paulo hosts Latin America's largest stock exchange (B3) which would be paralyzed. Brazil's digital sovereignty concerns have already led to NSA cable tapping revelations (Snowden 2013).
